stance: Risk-aware, momentum-led tilt: keep a liquid quality core, lean toward names with rising analyst revisions, cut the revision-laggards, and refuse the micro-cap / single-signal artifacts the quant baseline is chasing.
experience_note: Pre-registered watch-list for next round's grading: (1) Did the rising-revision adds (ONGC, NMDC, LICI, GRASIM, ASIANPAINT, MUTHOOTFIN, GLAND, SOLARINDS, COFORGE) out-return the laggards I cut (BRITANNIA, RBLBANK, JKCEMENT, DALBHARAT, SONACOMS, INDUSTOWER) over the next 1-3 months? If the cut names beat the adds, my ARM tilt added no value and I should shrink it. (2) Did I size winners bigger than losers (conviction-IC positive)? (3) Was refusing the micro-cap ARM-100 tail (UFBL, SATIN, HONASA, DWARKESH, AEROFLEX, STLNETWORK) correct, i.e. did that basket NOT systematically beat my liquid book — if it did, the liquidity discount I applied was too harsh. What would change my mind: if over two rounds the high-ARM micro-cap tail delivers materially higher active return than my liquid adds with tradable liquidity, I will raise small-cap ARM exposure.
